response.justification: Bogdan has strong and broad technical credentials spanning 15+ years, with genuine developer tooling experience (test frameworks, CI infrastructure, MCP generators) and staff-level technical leadership. However, the role is fundamentally a people management position, and the resume contains no evidence of direct people management, headcount responsibility, performance reviews, or org-level change leadership. The two Staff designations and workshop facilitation suggest peer mentoring at most. The current career gap and the absence of management experience make this a poor fit for a Level II Engineering Manager role.
strengths: Extensive developer tooling background including test runners (Trial), assertion libraries (Fluent-Asserts), CI/CD setup, and MCP frameworks — directly relevant to the engineering productivity domain
15+ years of polyglot software development across web, mobile, embedded, and high-performance systems, providing credible technical depth for guiding system design and code review
Track record of knowledge-sharing through workshops, conference talks, and open-source projects, indicating ability to develop engineers and communicate technical concepts
concerns: Zero stated people management experience — no mention of direct reports, hiring, performance management, or team building, which is the core requirement of this role
No evidence of owning or executing a multi-quarter technical roadmap at the team or organizational level; project ownership has been individual-contributor or solo-founder scope
Active career gap since October 2025 and short tenures at recent employers (6 months at re:cinq, ~2 years at Optilyz) raise continuity and commitment questions for a strategic leadership hire
